free_text: Cross sections and resonance integrals for capture and
fission in long-lived americium isotopes
Engl.transl.of J,AE,23,316,1967
Issue 4.
Thermal reactor WWR-M
Exact meaning of cadmium neutron spectrum limit was not
indicated. Cadmium neutron spectrum limit was supposed
to be equal to 0.4 eV
Table 2 of At.Energy,v.23,is.4,p.316,1967 .
= Table 1 of INDC(CCP)-1,p.51,1967
(M.M.) Correction of Reaction codes,
Subents 9-11 were added
Subent 003 was corrected.
Some misprints were corrected.
BIB information was updated.
Ref. INDC(CCP) was added.
Update for current rules and dictionaries.
Subent 003 was corrected.
STATUS was updated for new rules.
Samples were irradiated in neutron flux from
1.5E+13 to 1.0E+14 neutron/cm**2/sec.
A few micrograms of Am-241 (99.9 % of Am-241 and
0.1 % of Pu-239) on backing of beryllium or titanium
For determination of thermal cross sections
samples were irradiated with cadmium cover and without
one.
Irradiation time was within 5-100 hours
They were used for measuring of neutron flux
In COMMON section EN-MIN and EN-MAX of incident
spectrum were given
Type of uncertainty is not explained in the
articles.
PART-DET -> RAD-DET
Samples were irradiated in neutron flux from
1.5E+13 to 1.0E+14 neutron/cm**2/sec. Irradiation
time was within 5-100 hours. For determination
of Am-242-M quantity - 5.0E+7 neutron/cm**2/sec
neutron flux was used
A few micrograms of Am-241 (99.9 % of Am-241 and

samples were irradiated with cadmium cover and without
one
For determination of Am-242-M quantity
double fission chamber was used.
For samples alpha-activity measuring
For measuring of neutron flux - gold was used
For measuring of neutron flux - neptunium was used
ASSUM
Am-241 capture c-s was measured by accumulated Am-242m
number defined by Am-242m thermal fission c-s .
In COMMON section EN-MIN, EN-MAX of incident spectrum
and last fission cross section data of americium 242
in metastable state are given
Type of uncertainty is not explained in the
articles.
